pom.xml.general_idea: Update the project's target Java version property from 17 to 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the build targets Java 17, failing to enforce Java 21 language features and compatibility checks required for the migration.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated source/target compiler configuration with the unified release flag.
pom.xml.why_exists: The source and target options are deprecated in modern Java toolchains; using release ensures consistent language level and bootstrap classpath for Java 21.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add release flag to the test compiler plugin configuration.
pom.xml.why_exists: Ensures test classes are compiled with the correct Java 21 language level and bootstrap classpath, preventing runtime incompatibilities.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 14+ serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Compilation error: serialVersionUID must be annotated with @Serial in Java 14+ if the class implements Serializable.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Import java.io.Serial to support the @Serial annotation.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Compilation error: cannot find symbol @Serial without the import.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct semantic marking of serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the new annotation.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is defined in java.io.Serial; without the import, the code will fail to compile.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 17+ serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 17+ enforces that serialVersionUID fields in serializable classes must be annotated with @Serial (or be effectively final and private static final long) to avoid compile-time errors or warnings treated as errors in strict builds.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the @Serial annotation.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is part of java.io in Java 21; without the import, the code will not compile.
